group_1.textRight: What will you do now, Yakumo?

group_1.textLeft: If my wish will inevitably come true in the end...
And rain down destruction no matter@how hard I try...
Then I'll stop resisting.
I'll ride the waves of perdition and bring about@the future I asked for.

group_1.textRight: ...So you're going back to those days before, then...

group_1.textLeft: I am.@Although I once tried to find another way...
Do you want this city to be destroyed?

group_1.textRight: Can't you tell just from looking at my face?

group_1.textLeft: Yes, sorry. It was a stupid question.
We're Magical Girls, but first and foremost, we're members of human society.
There's no need to bear that any longer.
Ah, Kanagi...@Is now a good time?

group_1.textRight: I'm baaaack.

group_1.textLeft: Oh, and you're here too. Perfect timing.
Can I borrow your ears for a moment? I have@something important to discuss.

group_1.textRight: That works for me...
Did something happen?

group_1.textLeft: You know how Dad told you he was starting a new@job in Sankyo Ward next month...?

group_1.textRight: Yeah, I remember that.
I heard they didn't care that you had a limp@and hired you.
You worked hard and got your computer@certification, right?
I bet they recognized your efforts.

group_1.textLeft: Well, the company gave me a call.
I lost the position...

group_1.textRight: [chara:832400:effect_emotion_surprise_0][se:7226_shock]...?!

group_1.textLeft: Sorry...
I thought I was finally gonna be able to make things@easier for you guys...
I'm sorry for being such a deadbeat of a father...
We're still going to have some hard times ahead...

group_1.textRight: You're not a deadbeat at all.@Besides, we'll be alright.
I've been getting back into my maid job recently,@and I enjoy playing the role of Nagitan.

group_1.textLeft: Kanagi...

group_1.textRight: ...Why...why'd they turn you down?

group_1.textLeft: ...Your old man just wasn't competent enough.@Don't read too much into it.

group_1.textRight: It's because you're from the East, isn't it...?!

group_1.textLeft: If that were the case, they never would've offered@me the job in the first place.

group_1.textRight: No, I bet it was the last election.@It made the East look awful.

group_1.textLeft: Even if that's the reason,@it's not the manager's fault.
They're a small, locally-run business, after all...
There are all kinds of different viewpoints,@but a number of folks don't like the East...
Your old man would end up suffering if he went in.
The boss was worried about that too, and made@a choice from there...

group_1.textRight: It's like a headwind started blowing towards@the East.
Even those guys never used to be that insistent...

group_1.textLeft: What do you mean...?

group_1.textRight: Sorry. I got in a fight with a dude from the West.
He took a swing at me first,@and I know I shouldn't have,
but I fought back and punched him...

group_1.textLeft: Now's the time to be more patient. You're only@gonna feed a vicious cycle...

group_1.textRight: I get that...!@But I couldn't just stand there and take it!

group_1.textCenter: (I understand what they're saying... We truly are@caught in a vicious cycle...)
(Rumors that "our kind" are dangerous continue@to spread,)
(and people who think like that company's boss@are dwindling out...)
(As much as I hate the few people who instigated@all of this,)
(it's the history of Kamihama itself that perpetuates@the situation...)
